EnvelopeVersion.description: Envelope schema version. A newtype enum (rather than a free-form string) so adding a new variant is a compile-time event and so downstream deserializers can refuse unknown versions at parse time instead of accepting a string they can't actually handle.

FivetranColumnConfig.description: One column entry inside the per-table schema config.

enabled.description: Whether this column is enabled for sync.

hashed.description: Whether Fivetran is applying its column-hash transform on this column (PII masking flag).

FivetranConnectorStatus.description: Sync-status block on a connector. Two fields today; left as a struct rather than flattened so future Fivetran additions land without breaking the envelope shape.

FivetranConnectorSummary.description:
Per-connector summary projected into the envelope.

Strict superset of what `dagster_fivetran.FivetranWorkspaceData` consumes — the Python side can derive that shape from this one losslessly. A round-trip parity test against the dagster-fivetran public type lives on the Python side and is tracked as a follow-up.

failed_at.description: Last failed sync timestamp, if any.

group_id.description: Fivetran group ID owning this connector.

name.description: Human-friendly connector name. Fivetran returns this on the `groups/{id}/connectors` payload but the adapter currently captures only the `schema` field; the envelope surfaces both so downstream UIs can render the friendly name without re-querying. Falls back to `schema` when the upstream payload omits `name`.

schema.description: Destination schema this connector writes into.

status.description: Setup + sync state at fetch time.

succeeded_at.description: Last successful sync timestamp, if any.

FivetranDestination.description:
Fivetran destination metadata projected into the envelope.

Fields use the same names Fivetran's `GET /v1/destinations/{id}` endpoint returns — Rocky's adapter takes the wire shape verbatim so downstream consumers can move between the envelope and the upstream API without rename pain.

id.description: Fivetran destination identifier (e.g. `"dest_xyz"`). Globally unique within an account.

region.description: Region the destination is hosted in (e.g. `"us-east-1"`). Optional — older destination records may omit it.

service.description: Fivetran service tag for the destination warehouse (e.g. `"snowflake"`, `"big_query"`). Optional.

setup_status.description: Setup status of the destination, when present.

time_zone.description: Destination time zone (e.g. `"UTC"`). Optional for the same reason as `region`.

FivetranSchemaConfig.description: Schema config payload from `GET /v1/connectors/{id}/schemas`, projected into envelope shape with [`BTreeMap`]s for stable iteration order.

schemas.description: Map of logical schema key → schema entry.

FivetranSchemaEntry.description: One schema entry inside the per-connector schema config.

enabled.description: Whether this destination schema is enabled.

name_in_destination.description: Destination schema name when Fivetran has renamed it.

tables.description: Tables in this schema. [`BTreeMap`] for stable iteration order.

FivetranTableConfig.description: One table entry inside the per-connector schema config.

columns.description: Per-column config. [`BTreeMap`] for stable iteration order so the envelope hash doesn't drift on HashMap shuffles.

enabled.description: Whether this table is enabled for sync.

name_in_destination.description: Destination table name when Fivetran renames it (manual override or the `do_not_alter_` auto-prefix after a breaking schema change). The destination name is what shows up in `information_schema.tables`.

description:
Canonical Fivetran state envelope written by `rocky discover --emit-fivetran-state-to <PATH>`.

The envelope is the single contract between Rocky and downstream consumers that want a snapshot of the Fivetran view of a destination — connectors, their sync states, and the per-connector schema config — without re-fetching from Fivetran themselves.

## Construction invariants

- [`Self::connectors`] is sorted ascending by `connector.id` at construction time via [`Self::from_parts`]. - [`Self::schemas`] is a [`BTreeMap`] so iteration order is the ascending lexicographic order of `connector_id` keys.

These guarantees make the serialized bytes byte-stable across runs (modulo [`Self::fetched_at`]), which is what makes [`envelope_hash`] a useful sentinel for the idempotent emit path.

connectors.description: Connector summaries sorted ascending by `connector.id` — see the construction-invariant note on the type doc.

schemas.description: Schema config keyed by connector id. [`BTreeMap`] for stable iteration order.

title: FivetranStateEnvelope
